metadata.name: Sizzl Market Ontology
metadata.description: TrustGraph Ontology RAG projection of the canonical Sizzl market ontology.

SizzlEntity.rdfs:comment: Root class for all Sizzl market-ontology entities. Do not emit instances of this class directly; use one of the concrete subclasses below.

Market.rdfs:comment: A bounded competitive space in which Companies offer Offerings to StakeholderArchetypes — e.g. the smartphone market, the AI-platform market, the at-home baking market.

Stage.rdfs:comment: A discrete step in a buyer's decision journey through a Market — e.g. for SaaS: "awareness", "trial-signup", "trial-active", "upgrade", "renewal". Attributes can be relevantAt different Stages.

Transition.rdfs:comment: A move from one Stage to another, in scope of one Market, relevant to one StakeholderArchetype — e.g. trial-signup → trial-active, relevant to the price-sensitive small-business owner archetype. Captures funnel dynamics.

StakeholderArchetype.rdfs:comment: A type of buyer, user, decision-maker, or other stakeholder whose preferences shape decisions in a Market — e.g. a CTO evaluating an AI platform, a parent buying baking mix, a small-business owner choosing a payments processor.

Person.rdfs:comment: A natural person who plays one or more stakeholder-archetype roles in a company or market context — e.g. a CFO at Acme, a procurement lead, or an executive buyer. Use Person when individual identity matters; bind them to roles with plays.

Offering.rdfs:comment: A specific product or service a Company brings to a Market — e.g. iPhone Pro Max, IBM watsonx, Pillsbury Grands biscuits, Notion AI add-on.

Attribute.rdfs:comment: A specific dimension of an Offering that buyers compare on when choosing between products — e.g. price tier, ecosystem integration, ingredient quality, API latency, customer-support tier. Always tied to an Offering via hasAttribute.

AttributeLevel.rdfs:comment: A specific value an Attribute can take in a comparison — e.g. for Attribute 'price tier': levels 'premium', 'mid-market', 'value'; for Attribute 'API latency': levels '<100ms', '100-500ms', '>500ms'. Always tied to an Attribute via hasLevel.

Trait.rdfs:comment: A specific property of a StakeholderArchetype that drives their preferences — e.g. price-sensitivity, brand-loyalty, risk-tolerance, technical-fluency, time-on-platform. Always tied to a StakeholderArchetype via hasTrait.

TraitLevel.rdfs:comment: A specific value a Trait can take — e.g. for Trait 'price-sensitivity': levels 'high', 'medium', 'low'; for Trait 'technical-fluency': levels 'novice', 'intermediate', 'expert'. Always tied to a Trait via hasLevel.

Evidence.rdfs:comment: A claim grounded in a source, used to support or refute an ontology fact — e.g. "23% of Notion trial users convert to AI tier (Notion Q3 report)", "iPhone 15 Pro Max is $1199 at apple.com/shop". Always cites a sourceRef or sourceUrl.

Estimate.rdfs:comment: A numerical or qualitative prediction about an Offering, Market, or stakeholder behavior — e.g. "India iPhone market will reach 50M units by 2027", "AI conversion rate will double if pricing is bundled". Carries a confidence score and a value.

Company.rdfs:comment: A real-world commercial organization that offers products or services in a market — e.g. Apple, IBM, Pillsbury, OpenAI.

ExperimentRun.rdfs:comment: A specific test, study, or trial run to validate an Estimate or discover an Attribute — e.g. an A/B price test, a customer interview round, a usability study, a synthetic-respondent Subconscious.ai run. Has a date range and produces Estimates.

fromStage.rdfs:comment: Connects a transition to the stage it starts from.

toStage.rdfs:comment: Connects a transition to the stage it moves to.

inMarket.rdfs:comment: Connects a transition to its market scope.

relevantTo.rdfs:comment: Connects a transition to a relevant stakeholder archetype.

about.rdfs:comment: Connects a transition or estimate to the entity it describes.

hasAttribute.rdfs:comment: Connects an offering to one of its market attributes.

hasLevel.rdfs:comment: Connects an attribute or trait to a possible level.

hasTrait.rdfs:comment: Connects a stakeholder archetype to a trait.

relevantAt.rdfs:comment: Connects an attribute to the journey stage where it is relevant.

supports.rdfs:comment: Connects evidence to the ontology entity or claim it supports.

offeredBy.rdfs:comment: Connects an offering to its company.

competesWith.rdfs:comment: Connects one company to another company it directly competes with.

partneredWith.rdfs:comment: Connects companies with a strategic alliance, channel partnership, joint venture, supplier relationship, or co-marketing relationship.

acquired.rdfs:comment: Connects an acquiring company to the company it acquired.

producedBy.rdfs:comment: Connects an offering to the company that produces it. Downstream queries should treat producedBy and offeredBy as equivalent where appropriate.

alternativeTo.rdfs:comment: Connects offerings that buyers can treat as functional substitutes in a choice set.

complementOf.rdfs:comment: Connects offerings whose value increases when they are bundled or used together.

plays.rdfs:comment: Connects a person to a stakeholder archetype role they play in a company or market context.

consumed.rdfs:comment: Connects an experiment run to ontology context it consumed.

produced.rdfs:comment: Connects an experiment run to an estimate it produced.

sizzlId.rdfs:comment: Extraction property for canonical field id.

name.rdfs:comment: Extraction property for canonical field name.

definition.rdfs:comment: Extraction property for canonical field definition.

schemaVersion.rdfs:comment: Extraction property for canonical field schema_version.

validFrom.rdfs:comment: Extraction property for canonical field valid_from.

validTo.rdfs:comment: Extraction property for canonical field valid_to.

confidence.rdfs:comment: Extraction property for canonical field confidence.

sourceRef.rdfs:comment: Extraction property for canonical field source_ref.

sourceUrl.rdfs:comment: Extraction property for canonical field source_url.

extractedClaim.rdfs:comment: Extraction property for canonical field extracted_claim.
